steps.title: Identify slope direction
steps.content: The scatterplot trends downward: $y$ falls from about $10$ near $x=0$ to about $2$ near $x=10$. This rules out the positive-slope the other choices ($y=0.8+8.5x$) and C ($y=8.5+0.8x$).

steps.title: Compare the two negative-slope choices
steps.content:
The Desmos expressions below recreate representative points from the scatterplot and overlay choice D's line to check the fit.
  • Choice B — ‘$y=0.8-8.5x$’ $y = 0.8 - 8.5x$: $y$-intercept $0.8$ is far below the observed value near $10$, and slope $-8.5$ would drop $y$ by $85$ units across $0 \le x \le 10$. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$y=8.5-0.8x$’ $y = 8.5 - 0.8x$: $y$-intercept $8.5$ is close to the data near $x=0$, and slope $-0.8$ predicts a drop of $8$ units across the visible $x$-range, matching the trend. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
